steps.title: Identify the target of the participial phrase
steps.content: The opening participial phrase “Named in 1999 as one of the greatest achievements by a US chemist in the past hundred years” must modify the subject of the main clause. That subject must be the thing that was named: the synthesis, not Julian or the alkaloid compound.

steps.title: Apply the participial modifier rule
steps.content: C correctly places “Julian's 1935 synthesis of the alkaloid physostigmine” as the subject, so the participial phrase modifies what it refers to. A, B, and D all fail: A and B place “Julian” (misplacing the participle to the wrong noun), and D creates a compound predicate with mismatched voice. That matches choice C — C is correct.
